title: Noghri Death Commando

{{Quote|Oh, yes, Noghri—I know what you and your people are. The Emperor's private Death Commandos; killing and dying at the whim of ambitious men like Darth Vader and the Grand Admiral here.|[[Joruus C'baoth]]|<ref name="Heir" />}}
The '''Noghri Death Commandos''' were [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] that served as personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] for [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and her [[Skywalker family/Legends|family]] under the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]].

First formed as [[Imperial Special Forces/Legends|special forces]] in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] placed at the disposal of high-placed officials of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], such as [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] or [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]. Their main tasks were the abduction and assassination of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] personnel or the rescue of imprisoned Imperial officials.

==History==
{{Quote|Lord Vader has turned over to me command of a group of alien commandos who have proven themselves highly valuable to him over the years. While I won't have much use for them in the Unknown Regions, I have no doubt I'll eventually be returning to the Empire proper. At that time—well, we shall see what they can do.|[[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Dagon Niriz|Niriz]]|<ref name="Side Trip">"[[Side Trip]]"</ref>}}
[[File:NoghriDeathCommandos-DRF3.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A trio of Noghri Death Commandos]]
Noghri Death Commandos were first at the beck and call of the [[Imperial Military]]'s [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]],<ref name="kenner">{{KennerCite|set=Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (toy line)|pack=Darth Vader vs. Prince Xizor|link=soteKxizor-vader.asp}}</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], whom they regarded as the savior of their race. Their affiliation with [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] apprentice soon earned them notoriety as the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] private Death [[Commando/Legends|Commandos]].<ref name="Heir">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref>

[[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader also gave a squad of Noghri Death Commandos to [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Valin Draco]], as a favor for helping him interroge [[Halagad Ventor]]. Draco's [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] were later killed by agents of the [[Alderaanian Resistance]], while on a mission to the [[Almas Academy]].

Following [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] assisting of Darth Vader against [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor's]] criminal syndicate known as the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] rewarded the [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] by relinquishing command of the Noghri Death Commandos to him.<ref name="DFR Sourcebook">''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]''</ref>

After the death of Vader, the Noghri Death Commandos, along with the rest of the Noghri people, continued to serve the Empire, specifically Thrawn's forces. When the Grand Admiral targeted [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] for assassination, the Noghri Death Commandos partook in many attempts on her life. When the [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] revealed the Empire's treachery, the outraged Noghri turned against their former masters. Instead of seeking to take Leia's life, they acted as her loyal bodyguards, and it was essentially impossible to have them leave their mistress's side. A number of them were slain defending the lives of her and her family throughout the years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and into the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]].

==Equipment==
They used either the standard issue that was the [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]], as well as the [[T-21 light repeating blaster/Legends|T-21 light repeating blaster]] or a [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrower]] during their service. Some could use [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]], [[Spray stick/Legends|spray sticks]], and [[Vibroknife/Legends|knives]] to complete their work. It was rumored that they used [[Nightstinger sporting blaster rifle]]s during their collaboration with the Empire.
